note: Sprint 5.26 Trinity Task Queue v0.1: adds scripts/trinity/task_queue.py as the first step from manual one-shot operator runs toward autonomous operation. Local-dry-run only deterministic queue of Useful Compute requests under a queue directory (queue.json + pending/ + running/ + completed/ + failed/ + reports/). Six CLI subcommands: init, enqueue, list, run-once, inspect, validate. Schema schemas/trinity/task_queue.schema.json ($id=trinity-task-queue/v0.1) with the queue.json top-level and the queue_item shape under $defs/queue_item. queue_item carries schema (const trinity-task-queue-item/v0.1), queue_item_id (pattern ^qit-[0-9a-f]{16}$, deterministic sha16 of pinned_time+request_sha256+policy_sha256+worker_address_map_basename), request_json_path + worker_address_map_path + governor_policy_path (absolute paths needed for subprocess invocation) plus the same three _basename audit fields, status enum (pending/running/completed/failed), pinned_time, attempt_count, max_attempts bounded 1..16, last_error, operator_run_path, watchdog_report_path, policy_sha256 + request_sha256 (pinned at enqueue, 64-hex), threat_refs (^T[0-9]{2}$), governor_decisions_count, watchdog_safety_status (ok/warning/stale/critical or null). run-once picks the oldest pending item, invokes useful_compute_operator_loop.py via subprocess with --mode local-dry-run, --require-confirmation-token I_UNDERSTAND_THIS_IS_ONLY_A_DRY_RUN_LOOP and --governor-policy (always), then invokes governor_watchdog.py over the resulting decisions dir. Fail-closed contract: operator_loop rc=3 (governor hard-block: halt_file_present or policy_mutated_at_runtime) marks the item failed with last_error=governor_hard_block, no retry inside run-once; watchdog safety_status=critical marks the item failed even when operator_loop returned rc=0; any other non-zero rc marks the item failed with the operator output tail in last_error. Sibling scripts invoked argv-only, never shell — static safety test rejects shell=True plus all wallet/private_key/seed_phrase/mnemonic/passphrase/ecdsa/secp256k1/sign_tx/sign_transaction/real_sign/sendrawtransaction/broadcast/sost-cli/sost_cli tokens plus every stdlib network primitive (urlopen, urllib.request, requests.post, http.client, socket.socket) and rejects sibling-module imports (autonomy_governor, governor_watchdog, useful_compute_operator_loop, task_builder, worker). Cross-check safety re-asserts that Sprint 5.26 did NOT regress Governor or Watchdog forbidden-token surfaces. queue runner explicitly references and enforces ALLOWED_MODE='local-dry-run' and GOVERNOR_HARD_BLOCK_RC=3. Tests: 1090 passed, 37 skipped on the deployment host (1046 prior + 44 new = 19 functional + 14 schema + 11 safety). Manual demo: init + enqueue + run-once + list completes one scientific_intake request with governor_decisions_count=7, watchdog_safety_status=ok, threat_refs=[T15,T16,T17]. Non-goals for v0.1 (documented): no daemon, no retries (max_attempts recorded but not consumed), no priorities (strict FIFO by created_at), no concurrency (atomic queue.json write narrows race but no proper locking), no remote queue, no autonomous re-enqueue, no wallet/signing/broadcasting/real-payment in any code path. Pure scripts+schemas+docs+tests merge: zero src/, zero consensus changes, zero wallet/payment/broadcast changes.
